name: Forgejo package registry (PyPI)
cost: Free (FOSS, GPLv3+ since v9.0; pre-v9.0 was MIT). No paid tiers from the project. Codeberg.org runs a free public Forgejo instance.
perf_notes: No published PyPI-registry-specific benchmarks. Single Go binary with a light footprint (runs on a Raspberry Pi / small VPS); the v15 metrics rework trims default Prometheus series. Package blobs are content-addressed with built-in deduplication, so identical uploads share storage. Throughput/latency for the package API are gated by the chosen DB (SQLite/MySQL/Postgres) and storage backend (local disk vs S3/MinIO).
scale_notes: Architecture is one Forgejo binary + a relational DB + optional Redis/memcache (cache/sessions/queues) + a storage backend; packages can live on shared S3/MinIO. Multiple stateless frontends are possible behind shared DB/storage/cache, but background cron jobs (incl. cleanup_packages) and some operations assume a single primary, so true HA/clustering is community-supported rather than an officially blessed design. Codeberg.org is a large public Forgejo instance demonstrating real-world scale.
summary: A self-hosted Git forge whose built-in PyPI registry gives each user/org a private Python index (PEP 503 + PEP 691) with twine upload, inheriting the forge's auth, RBAC and object storage, but with no upstream proxying or mirroring.
extras: Part of a ~24-ecosystem package registry (npm, Maven, Container/OCI, Conda, Cargo, NuGet, Debian, RPM, Alpine, Helm, Go, Pub, Swift, Vagrant, Generic, etc.) sharing one auth/storage/quota layer
Packages belong to an owner (user or organization) namespace at /api/packages/{owner}/pypi, not to a repo
Owner-configurable cleanup rules (keep latest N versions, remove versions older than X, regex match/exclude) run by a nightly cron.cleanup_packages job
Built-in deduplication of uploaded blobs across packages (one blob stored for identical files)
Per-upload quota / size limits enforced on the upload handler
JSON Simple API (application/vnd.pypi.simple.v1+json) advertises api-version 1.4 and includes a top-level versions array plus per-file size and sha256, but no upload-time (so PEP 700 only partial)

Simple HTML index emits sha256 in the file href fragment (#sha256=... — the PEP 503-compliant '=' form, fixed in v7.0.0 / issue #3189) and data-requires-python, but no PEP 629 pypi:repository-version meta tag and no data-dist-info-metadata/data-yanked attributes

Forgejo Actions (CI) jobs can publish to the registry using the in-job token; v15.0 added OIDC identity tokens for Actions (Forgejo acting as an OIDC issuer for external systems), but the registry itself does not consume an OIDC token for PyPI-style trusted publishing
Package storage backends are local filesystem or S3-compatible via the MinIO driver only — no native Google Cloud Storage or Azure Blob backend
REST API to list/inspect packages (/api/v1/packages/{owner}, with q/type filters) but no PyPI XML-RPC/Warehouse-style search
Single self-contained Go binary; can run with embedded SQLite and no external DB server
Personal access tokens carry read:package / write:package scopes; org teams grant package read/write/admin; v15.0 added repo-restricted access tokens
GPLv3+ since v9.0 (Aug 2024); all releases before v9.0 were MIT-licensed
